pairs.question: Who was the Premier of the first Dibbs ministry in New South Wales?
pairs.answer: George Dibbs was the Premier of the first Dibbs ministry in New South Wales. He led the ministry, which was the 21st ministry of the Colony of New South Wales.

pairs.question: When did the title of Premier become a formal position in the government of New South Wales?
pairs.answer: The title of Premier became a formal position in the government of New South Wales in 1920.

pairs.question: What portfolio did George Dibbs hold in the first Dibbs ministry?
pairs.answer: George Dibbs held the portfolio of Colonial Treasurer in the first Dibbs ministry.

pairs.question: Why were ministers in the New South Wales Legislative Assembly required to resign and recontest their seats upon appointment before 1887?
pairs.answer: Before 1887, there was no party system in New South Wales politics. Ministers were required to resign and recontest their seats in a by-election to ensure their continued support from the electorate.

pairs.question: What event led to George Dibbs becoming Premier of New South Wales?
pairs.answer: George Dibbs became Premier following the resignation of Sir Alexander Stuart on 7 October 1885 due to ill-health.

pairs.question: How long did the first Dibbs ministry last, and what major issue led to its downfall?
pairs.answer: The first Dibbs ministry lasted from 7 October 1885 until 21 December 1885. It fell due to a projected budget deficit of over £1 million, despite Dibbs' attempts to govern.

pairs.question: Which two ministers were appointed to the first Dibbs ministry and were re-elected unopposed?
pairs.answer: Thomas Slattery (representing Boorowa) and William Lyne (representing The Hume) were the two new ministers appointed to the first Dibbs ministry and were re-elected unopposed.
